name: Steady-state Barrier / Handoff Retime

summary.name: Steady-state Barrier / Handoff Retime
summary.hypothesis: Round 10 forced an explicit audit of which queued families are still actionable versus already absorbed in the current source. After filtering out the sibling export-trim no-op and then measuring a real PTX control-path fallback, the latest run still regressed to 24.19046402 ms while the headline signature barely moved: active warps stayed at 16.61, barrier stayed at 5.46, and long-scoreboard stayed at 7.24. That combination means neither another export-side replay nor another trivial prologue-order nudge is the next best bet. The cleanest remaining unabsorbed lever on the current PTX winner is a narrower retime of the steady-state wait-group and handoff cadence around `cp.async`, `__syncthreads()`, and the future-tile refill path. It keeps the accepted PTX surface intact while attacking the scheduler seam that is still visibly exposed after the export cleanup and the latest control-path tweak.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Residual wait-group and barrier cadence in the PTX hot-band steady-state loop, especially the handoff between the current stage's MMA issue and the future-tile refill sequence.
summary.risk: Moderate. The family is bounded and still unabsorbed, but the current source already contains the strongest obvious one-sync cleanup, so another retime can easily reshuffle barrier versus scoreboard without creating a real runtime win.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164352 ms best-known PTX run
ranking_notes: This rises to rank 1 mainly because the round-10 audit proved several higher-priority-looking families were already absorbed and would only produce no-op node_c rounds.
It is the highest-priority open frontier family that is both unabsorbed and still aligned with the latest flat scheduler signature.
It stays ahead of restore-style fallbacks because it keeps the current PTX best surface live instead of stepping back onto a historically slower branch.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the handoff retime lowers barrier only by raising scoreboard enough to keep runtime flat, or if the wait-group shuffle produces no measurable hot-band time change.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
